_comment: sovereign-os router dashboard (R495, SDD-011+ R161 R215). Visualizes the operator-§1g inference router — the OpenAI-compatible front for the Trinity stack. Routing decisions are surfaced as Layer-B metrics: per-tier route counts, per-task-type classification (R161), per-model-class classification (R215), last-route freshness. Operator imports into Grafana via Settings → JSON Model. Closes the router dashboard:FUTURE waiver and extends router surface-map to 5 surfaces (core/cli/api/service/dashboard).

panels.title: last route age (s)
panels.description: Operator-§1g freshness signal — staleness of last routing decision. Red >1h, yellow >5m.

panels.title: task-types observed
panels.description: Distinct task_type labels seen (R161). 4-class operator-§1g taxonomy: code / math / conversational / creative.

panels.title: model-classes observed
panels.description: Distinct R215 model-class labels seen. 13-class operator-§1g taxonomy: llm/slm/rlm/ternary-lm/lora-adapter/embed/vision/multimodal/code/mixture/speculative/reranker/(unspecified).

panels.title: route rate per tier (5m)
panels.description: Per-tier router decisions over time. The operator-revealed-preference signal for tier demand.

panels.title: route rate per task-type (5m)
panels.description: Per-task-type rate (R161). 4-class taxonomy — code / math / conversational / creative — operator's revealed task-mix.

panels.title: route rate per model-class (5m)
panels.description: Per-R215-class rate. 13-class operator-§1g taxonomy. Answers: 'what fraction of traffic asked for class=rlm and routed to oracle?'

panels.title: cumulative routes per tier
panels.description: Sum-since-start per tier. Operator-§1g lifetime demand distribution.

panels.title: router decision matrix (tier × task-type)
panels.description: Cross-tab of tier × task_type since metrics start. Operator-discoverable: 'which task-types route to which tier?'

options.content:
**Operator §1g verbatim** (sacrosanct):

> "We do not minimize anything."

The router (SDD-011) is the operator-§1g OpenAI-compatible front for the Trinity stack. Every inference request enters via the router, gets classified (R161 task_type + R215 model_class), routed to a Trinity tier (pulse / logic-engine / oracle-core / llama_old / llama_fb), and the routing decision is surfaced as Layer-B metrics + as the `X-Sovereign-Task-Type` response header.

**R161 task-type taxonomy (operator-§1g, sacrosanct):**

1. `code` — programming, code-gen, code-review requests
2. `math` — mathematical reasoning, symbolic computation
3. `conversational` — chat-style multi-turn dialogue
4. `creative` — text-generation requiring creative latitude

**R215 model-class taxonomy (R212 catalog enum, 13 classes):**

1. `llm` — large language model (frontier-class)
2. `slm` — small language model
3. `rlm` — reasoning language model (chain-of-thought specialized)
4. `ternary-lm` — ternary-quantized LM (Pulse via bitnet.cpp)
5. `lora-adapter` — LoRA adapter on a base model
6. `embed` — embedding model
7. `vision` — vision-only model
8. `multimodal` — vision+text model
9. `code` — code-specialized model
10. `mixture` — mixture-of-experts
11. `speculative` — speculative-decoding draft model
12. `reranker` — retrieval reranker
13. `(unspecified)` — operator didn't supply `sovereign_os_class`

Class × tier cross-tabbed in metrics so the operator can answer: 'what fraction of traffic asked for class=rlm and routed to oracle?'

**Signal surfaces (operator-§1g end-to-end):**

1. Metric: `sovereign_os_inference_route_total{tier}` (Layer B)
2. Metric: `sovereign_os_inference_router_task_type_total{task_type}` (R161)
3. Metric: `sovereign_os_inference_router_class_total{class}` (R215)
4. Metric: `sovereign_os_inference_router_last_route_timestamp` (freshness gauge)
5. Response header: `X-Sovereign-Task-Type: <class>` (per-request)
6. Log line: `route: model=%r → tier=%s task_type=%s class=%s` (per-request)

**CLI:**

```
sovereign-osctl router status                # tier health + last-route
sovereign-osctl router metrics               # snapshot of all Layer-B
sovereign-osctl router classify --body @r.json  # dry-run classification
```

**Source:** SDD-011 shipped the router + R161 added task_type classification + R215 added model-class cross-tabbing. R495 (SDD-011+ R161 R215) ships THIS dashboard + extends router surface-map registration to 5 surfaces (core/cli/api/service/dashboard).

**Standing operator rule (sacrosanct):**

> "We do not minimize anything."

title: sovereign-os router
